author: @ssholee
text:
TOP COMMENT ON EDPWATCH 

robinbugs
18d

To everyone,
As we all know, there's been a severe lack of communication from Jidions team about the current state of EDPWatch.
I believe that Jidion should've given us all some notice before slowing down his uploads on this particular kind of content. The lack of communication has been really shocking in my opinion. A recent example similar to this situation that i can think of is when Jidion went on vacation a month or so back. We had no idea what had happened and, while i do understand that he and his team has a life outside of pred catches, the only way people were able to find out about his vacation/break was through other people speculating or by scrolling endlessly on one of his YouTube comments that very briefly addressed his absence.
I think that as we are paying subscribers, we deserve at least a little bit of communication about the current state of things or about his plans for this organization going forward. I absolutely love this kind of content and have huge amounts of respect for all of those involved that contribute to making the world a better place, however it does feel a bit disingenuous (intentional or not) that so many of us are seemingly left in the dark while we continue to pay forward on something that has a big cloud of uncertainty surrounding it at the moment.
All i ask of you, Jidion, is that we get a bit more communication about things. Whether it be a community post on here, YouTube or even on one of your instagram pages. A lot of people are clearly upset that we haven't been informed about any plans regarding EDPWatch.
And to everyone flaming Jidion, I beg of you to keep things civil and friendly. I understand completely that we are all upset and im right there with you, but there are genuine points we can bring up while remaining respectful. thanks

text: I feel like Charlie brings up a good point at the end of the video. It really does feel like, over time, on the internet, when bad things happen or when people do straight-up deplorable things, it never feels like the community has this almost unanimous outrage over it like it used to years before. Like there were always contrarians. But instead of it being a few people saying unhinged stuff from spam accounts. It became a few communities with full of unhinged people, and now it’s a few unhinged influencers with their own unhinged communities that end up creating these unhinged pockets of the internet. It just feels like societal taboos and common sense immoral behavior just don’t exist on the internet anymore and I feel like the older I get the more I see that bleed into real life.

author: @CuzinsAgainstPreds
text:
I respect the work you do, and I think your intentions are good. However, there are times when your approach toward law enforcement can come across as unprofessional, particularly during moments of frustration. While I understand the situation may be emotional, maintaining composure and professionalism generally strengthens your credibility and the effectiveness of your message.

I also believe your introductions could be presented in a more professional manner. First impressions matter, especially when addressing serious allegations or attempting to provide evidence.

From a legal standpoint, officers are often limited in what they can immediately do with the information provided. Even if they receive evidence, they typically require a warrant before they can legally search a phone. In many cases, the device must first be confiscated and processed as evidence, which can initiate an entirely separate investigation that may take a significant amount of time.

Once investigators are legally authorized to examine the contents of the phone, they can then review the evidence, establish probable cause, and proceed with charges or an arrest if sufficient proof exists. Because of this process, immediate action is not always possible, even when the allegations are serious.
